raw.title: Aileé Zaga + Bo Jahura
raw.description:
Join us for an intimate evening of bold art pop and heartfelt folk songwriting at Frequency Sound 528. This concert series night brings together two distinctive voices whose music blends vulnerability, atmosphere, and emotional depth.

Aileé Zaga

Aileé Zaga is a BC-based art pop songwriter and producer influenced by boundary-pushing artists like Björk and Imogen Heap. Blending electronic, classical, and alternative elements, she creates immersive soundscapes layered with memorable melodies and contemplative lyrics exploring identity, womanhood, and immigration.

A 2024 Best of Kelowna nominee for “Best Local Musician” and “Overall YouTube Channel,” Zaga’s 2023 single Written in the Earth received national praise. With over 125K YouTube views and performances alongside artists such as Luke Wallace and Locarno, she continues carving out a powerful presence in the Okanagan music scene.

Bo Jahura

Originally from BC’s West Coast and now based in the Okanagan, Bo Jahura crafts soulful, original folk music with sincerity and depth. Drawing from lived experience — from darker chapters to bright, hopeful days — his songs are honest, reflective, and emotionally resonant. Bo’s performances linger long after the final note fades.

This special fundraiser supports CNIWT Camp, which brings together families of Deaf, deafblind, and Hard of Hearing children from across the BC Interior for a barrier-free, inclusive camp experience. raw.title: Comedy for a Cause
raw.description:
Get ready for a powerful night of laughter in support of an incredible cause as Comedy for a Cause for CNIWT Camp.

This special fundraiser supports CNIWT Camp, which brings together families of Deaf, deafblind, and Hard of Hearing children from across the BC Interior for a barrier-free, inclusive camp experience. The camp includes ASL interpreters, Deaf and Hard of Hearing leaders and role models, resource professionals, and parent workshops focused on supporting their children.

The night will feature a stacked lineup of Kelowna Comedy’s top performers, with a special performance by Joey Commisso.

Join us for a night filled with big laughs and real impact. Every ticket helps send families to camp.

The camp includes ASL interpreters, Deaf and Hard of Hearing leaders and role models, resource professionals, and parent

raw.title: Comedy for a Cause for CNIWT Family Camp presented by Whitetail Concrete
raw.description:
Get ready for a powerful night of laughter in support of an incredible cause as Comedy for a Cause for CNIWT Camp, presented by Whitetail Concrete, comes to Dakoda’s Comedy Lounge.
This special fundraiser supports CNIWT Camp, which brings together families of Deaf, deafblind, and Hard of Hearing children from across the BC Interior for a barrier-free, inclusive camp experience. The camp includes ASL interpreters, Deaf and Hard of Hearing leaders and role models, resource professionals, and parent workshops focused on supporting their children.
The night will feature a stacked lineup of Kelowna Comedy’s top performers, with a special performance by Joey Commisso.
Joey is one of Western Canada’s fastest-rising stand-up comics. His debut album and special Nachosferatu (2025) was filmed to sold-out crowds and released through Comedy Here Often?. He has performed multiple times at Just For Laughs Vancouver and was selected for NBC’s prestigious StandUp NBC showcase. A Vancouver Comedy Award nominee, Joey tours relentlessly across Canada, the United States, and Europe with high-energy material about fatherhood and his “Fake Italian” life. His viral clips and packed club shows have made him a fan favorite wherever he performs.
Join us for a night filled with big laughs and real impact. Every ticket helps send families to camp.
Thanks to the generous sponsorship of Whitetail Concrete, 100% of ticket sales will go directly to this cause. Whitetail Concrete is committed to showing up on time, standing behind their work, and making sure every customer feels taken care of from start to finish

raw.title: Devon Coyote Trio Live
raw.description:
Roots grit. Soul grooves. Rock & roll that actually moves you.

This live music event brings a double dose of groove-driven energy to Red Bird as the Devon Coyote Trio takes the stage with Nico opening the night.

The Devon Coyote Trio deliver raw, electrifying rock & roll with a chemistry you can feel from the first note. This collective of long-time friends blends Roots, Rock, Blues and beyond into a sound that feels both nostalgic and fresh. Their live shows are high-energy without being overworked — powerful musicianship, tight grooves, and that laid-back, down-to-earth vibe that pulls the whole room in. It’s the kind of night where the band is locked in, the crowd is locked in, and the connection carries from stage to the floor.

Nico

Opening the night is Kelowna-based artist Nico — a smooth, genre-blending force of funky-folk and jazzy-soul. Equal parts groove and glow, Nico mixes original music with soulful reimaginings of 70s classics that make you want to move without even thinking about it. Having played everywhere from European streets to North American festivals, Nico brings polish, personality, and just the right amount of swagger to set the tone for the evening.

This is live music the way it’s meant to be: honest, soulful, and impossible to stand still through.

title: Eagle Eyes: The Tribute to The Eagles
description: "Eagles dominated soft rock throughout the 70’s with unforgettable hits like Take It Easy, Witchy Woman, Best Of My Love, One of These Nights, Lyin’ Eyes, Take It To The Limit, New Kid In Town. Hotel California, Life In The Fast Lane, Heartache Tonight, The Long Run, I Can’t Tell You Why… and more.Beautiful and catchy melodies with amazing lead and harmony vocals have made the Eagles one of the most famous, respected and loved rock groups of all time, with broad appeal to all ages. Eagle Eyes ca

raw.title: Eagle Eyes: The Tribute to The Eagles
raw.description:
"Eagles dominated soft rock throughout the 70’s with unforgettable hits like Take It Easy, Witchy Woman, Best Of My Love, One of These Nights, Lyin’ Eyes, Take It To The Limit, New Kid In Town. Hotel California, Life In The Fast Lane, Heartache Tonight, The Long Run, I Can’t Tell You Why… and more.

Beautiful and catchy melodies with amazing lead and harmony vocals have made the Eagles one of the most famous, respected and loved rock groups of all time, with broad appeal to all ages. Eagle Eyes capture lead and harmony vocals with breath-taking precision that is nothing less than stunning. All vocals are live…. you won’t hear a single pre-recorded vocal track and no auto-tune.

Eagle Eyes is the best Eagles tribute in North America, and has received standing ovations from audiences in Canada, and in several States in the USA, including Washington, Oregon, California, Arizona, Idaho and Alaska Eagle Eyes are… Jack Gunderson, bass and lead vocals, fills the vocal roles of Glenn Frey and Timothy B. Schmidt. Jason Dunajski, guitars and lead vocals, fills the vocal roles of Don Henley and Randy Meisner. Ray Harvey, guitars and lead vocals, fills the vocal role of Joe Walsh. Kenneth Ermter, drums and lead vocals, also fills the vocal roles of Henley and Meisner.

All four members of Eagle Eyes are lifetime professional musicians with several decades of combined experience on the road and in the studio with a number of different bands and projects over the years. Eagle Eyes embrace all of this musical dedication and experience to deliver a show that is nothing short of magnificent, making them among the top-drawing tribute acts wherever they perform."

raw.title: Fireside Warm-Up: Wine Tasting, Smores & Mulled Wine
raw.description:
Join us for an exceptional experience and immerse yourself in the charm of our artisan farm!

With the changing of the seasons, this in-person event promises a warm and cozy atmosphere. You can enjoy the farm, indulge in delicious treats, and experience the scenic beauty of East Kelowna.

Experience an informative guided wine tasting, followed by a cozy and unwinding sesh by the fire. Savour the perfect pairing of warm mulled wine and s'mores you've roasted on the fire!

Includes:

  • Guided wine tasting (or Mocktails!)
  • Everything you need to roast S'mores over the fire pits!
  • A glass of our famous Mulled Mead (or homemade hot chocolate) 
  • Enter to win our winter basket

*Mocktails & Hot chocolate available for any non-drinkers or persons under 19 years

No physical ticket? Please just show the booking upon arrival.

*Please note that in accordance with BC Liquor laws, participants must show 2 pieces of ID for Wine tasting/Mulled Wine.*

title: Fireworks Show at Big White
description: Kick off winter evenings with a spectacular show at Big White.Fireworks will light up the sky every Saturday at 7 p.m., from Nov. 29 through April 4. The season opens with a special show in the Village Centre on Saturday, Nov. 29. After opening night, weekly fireworks move to Happy Valley, where the magic continues all winter. Whether you watch from your accommodation, a favourite restaurant or patio, or at the Happy Valley Skating Rink, each show will fill the night sky with vibrant colour and

raw.title: Fireworks Show at Big White
raw.description:
Kick off winter evenings with a spectacular show at Big White.
Fireworks will light up the sky every Saturday at 7 p.m., from Nov. 29 through April 4. The season opens with a special show in the Village Centre on Saturday, Nov. 29. After opening night, weekly fireworks move to Happy Valley, where the magic continues all winter. Whether you watch from your accommodation, a favourite restaurant or patio, or at the Happy Valley Skating Rink, each show will fill the night sky with vibrant colour and sparkling light. On Christmas Eve, fireworks return to the Village Centre for one special night to celebrate the holiday season.

title: Floor Opener
description: Lawrence Paul Yuxweluptun is one of Canada’s most outspoken and influential contemporary artists, confronting colonial suppression, environmental degradation, and the ongoing struggles for Indigenous sovereignty. Of Cowichan (Hul’q’umi’num Coast Salish) and Okanagan (Syilx) descent, his forty-year practice brings together traditional iconography, fearless critique, and biting satire to challenge and unsettle. Floor Opener presents a selection of works that are at once polemical and playful, imbu

raw.title: Floor Opener
raw.description: Lawrence Paul Yuxweluptun is one of Canada’s most outspoken and influential contemporary artists, confronting colonial suppression, environmental degradation, and the ongoing struggles for Indigenous sovereignty. Of Cowichan (Hul’q’umi’num Coast Salish) and Okanagan (Syilx) descent, his forty-year practice brings together traditional iconography, fearless critique, and biting satire to challenge and unsettle. Floor Opener presents a selection of works that are at once polemical and playful, imbued with a spiritual resonance that speaks urgently to the political and social realities of our time. This nationally significant exhibition is organized and circulated by the Kitchener-Waterloo Art Gallery, with artwork sourced from private collections in British Columbia.
